steps.title: Match comfort with understanding
steps.content: The claim says Alexandra takes comfort in understanding the world around her. The strongest quotation must do more than show that she likes nature or faces hardship bravely. It needs to connect her emotional comfort to a sense that the natural world has order, structure, or laws she can reflect on and understand.

steps.title: Eliminate incomplete evidence
steps.content:
• Choice A: Shows Alexandra gathering strength to face a painful situation. That is determination under pressure, not comfort that comes from understanding nature.
  • Choice B: Shows how much the country means to Alexandra, with insects, birds, and wild things around her. It shows attachment to the land but not security through understanding the world's order.

steps.title: Choose the ordered nature quote
steps.content:
• Choice C: Describes Alexandra driving alone into the dark country. It gives no evidence of personal security or reflection on natural order.
Choice D describes Alexandra watching the stars, thinking about their vastness, distance, and ordered march, and feeling fortified by the law behind nature. That directly illustrates comfort through understanding. The answer is D.
